recent_coach_change: Yes. Pape Thiaw was appointed on 13 December 2024, replacing Aliou Cisse who had been dismissed about two months earlier.
qualification_path: Won CAF Group B unbeaten with 24 points from 30 (seven wins, three draws), finishing two points ahead of DR Congo; sealed top spot with a 4-0 win over Mauritania.
key_players: Sadio Mane (Al-Nassr) — attacking talisman, scored a double against Mauritania to seal qualification
Kalidou Koulibaly (Al-Hilal) — captain and defensive leader
Idrissa Gana Gueye (Everton) — veteran midfield anchor
Nicolas Jackson (Chelsea) — forward providing European-club firepower
squad_notes: Final 26 trimmed from a 28-man preliminary list; Ilay Camara and Moustapha Mbow were the two players cut after the USA friendly. Malang Sarr was omitted, with Thiaw explaining the decision publicly. Core of the 2022 AFCON-winning golden generation, several of whom are likely at their final World Cup.
warmup_assessment: Underwhelming. Senegal lost 3-2 to the United States on 31 May 2026, with a wobbly defensive display, and faced Saudi Arabia on 9 June. Preparations were further disrupted by an off-pitch contract standoff involving coach Pape Thiaw.
shape_verdict: A talented, experienced squad with one of the deepest player pools at the tournament, but defensive frailty in the USA friendly and coaching-contract turmoil cloud their build-up.

news.headline: USA beat Senegal 3-2 in World Cup warm-up as Senegal defence wobbles
Senegal preparations thrown into chaos amid Pape Thiaw contract standoff
Senegal finalize squad, parting ways with Ilay Camara and Moustapha Mbow
